Sloka 29,—When the Sun, Jupiter and Venus occupy together a single house, the person born will be liberal in his gifts and he will be a renowned king.
Varahamihira: When the Moon occupies Krittika, the person may be a glutton, is fond of the spouses of others, has a bright appearance, and possesses widespread fame.
When either the lagna or the Moon occupies Krittika, ambition, pride and self-motivated qualities often appear in the native's personality.

While the ascendant or the Moon occupying Rohini often indicates a critical or fault-finding nature, a slim physique, and a fondness for the opposite sex, people with the Moon or Venus in Rohini rising in the lagna often have great charm, beauty and charisma, as well as ability or strong attraction for the arts.
Similar results may occur when Moon or Venus appears well-placed in Rohini as the lagna lord.
When the lagna or the Moon occupies Ardra, the native may have strong feelings which may be overwhelming.
